title: Verilator $sscanf reads into array with a delay
body:
I am experiencing strange behavior when trying to read lines from a file into an array one at a time.  The file has three lines:
10
11
12

When I run the testbench in Questa, I read them as expected.

```
harris@chips:~/cvw/examples/verilog/testcase$ qrun testbench.sv
...
# Read vectors.tv
# sig32[0] = 00000010   line: 00000010
# 
# sig32[1] = 00000011   line: 00000011
# 
# sig32[2] = 00000012   line: 00000012
# 
# ** Note: $finish    : testbench.sv(42)
```

But when I run it in Verilator, the first line shows up in the next array entry.

```
harris@chips:~/cvw/examples/verilog/testcase$ verilator --timescale "1ns/1ns" --timing --binary --top-module testbench testbench.sv
...
harris@chips:~/cvw/examples/verilog/testcase$ ./obj_dir/Vtestbench
Read vectors.tv
sig32[0] = **00000000**   line: 00000010

sig32[1] = 00000010   line: 00000011

sig32[2] = 00000011   line: 00000012

- testbench.sv:42: Verilog $finish
```

If I change the code to read into a temporary variable first, and then copy that variable into the array, Verilator matches Questa and my expectation.  This is done by commenting out line 31 and uncommenting lines 32 and 33.

```
harris@chips:~/cvw/examples/verilog/testcase$ verilator --timescale "1ns/1ns" --timing --binary --top-module testbench testbench.sv
...
harris@chips:~/cvw/examples/verilog/testcase$ ./obj_dir/Vtestbench
Read vectors.tv
sig32[0] = 00000010   line: 00000010
sig32[1] = 00000011   line: 00000011
sig32[2] = 00000012   line: 00000012
- testbench.sv:42: Verilog $finish
```

Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)

See attached testbench.sv and vectors.tv. (remove .txt suffix)
[vectors.tv.txt](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/13854177/vectors.tv.txt)
[testbench.sv.txt](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/13854178/testbench.sv.txt)

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

see above

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?

harris@chips:~/cvw/examples/verilog/testcase$ verilator --version
Verilator 5.021 devel rev v5.020-16-g7507c5f56

What OS and distribution are you using?

Ubuntu 22.04LTS

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

Not my strength.
